drivers.name: Daryl De Leon
drivers.bio:
Daryl De Leon is an Irish racing driver born on 2 August 2005 in Drogheda, Ireland. He made a stunning debut in the British Touring Car Championship in 2025, joining West Surrey Racing to drive a BMW 330e M Sport.

De Leon made history as the first Filipino driver to win a BTCC race, taking victory at Brands Hatch Indy Circuit in 2025. He went on to dominate the Jack Sears Trophy — the award for drivers yet to score more than one podium in the series — claiming the title with two pole positions and a win across the season.

His impressive maiden campaign earned him a second season with WSR, where he will look to build on his promising start and challenge further up the overall drivers' championship standings.

drivers.name: Tom Chilton
drivers.bio:
Tom Chilton is a British touring car driver born on 15 March 1985 in Reigate, England. He made his debut in the British Touring Car Championship in 2002 at just 17 years old, becoming one of the youngest drivers in the series. Over his BTCC career, Chilton has taken multiple race wins and podiums, regularly finishing as a front-running contender.

He later competed in the World Touring Car Championship from 2012, where he achieved several victories and a best championship finish of fifth in 2013.

Known for his consistency and longevity in touring car racing, Chilton has raced for a variety of teams and manufacturers, including Honda, Vauxhall, Ford, and Chevrolet.

history.team: Bristol Street Motors with Excelr8 TradePriceCars.com
Team Shredded Wheat Racing with Gallagher
Team Shredded Wheat Racing with Gallagher

drivers.name: Ricky Collard
drivers.bio:
Ricky Collard is a British racing driver born on 30 July 1996 in Surrey, the son of former BTCC driver Rob Collard. He made his BTCC debut in 2018 as a substitute for his injured father at West Surrey Racing, immediately impressing with a podium finish at Silverstone.

After stepping away from touring cars to pursue GT racing, Collard returned to the BTCC in 2022 with Speedworks Motorsport aboard the Toyota Corolla, completing two full seasons and finishing a career-best 8th in the 2023 championship as the leading Toyota driver.

In 2024 Collard won the British GT Championship GT3 title alongside his father Rob in a Lamborghini Huracán for Barwell Motorsport — becoming one of the few father-and-son pairs to win the same championship. He returns to the BTCC for 2026 with the title-winning Team VERTU operation, the most competitive drive of his touring car career.

history.team: Toyota GAZOO Racing UK with Speedworks
Toyota GAZOO Racing UK with Speedworks

drivers.name: Lewis Selby
drivers.bio:
Despite ironically standing at more than six foot, Lewis Selby has become synonymous for competing in MINIs, both classic and modern.

Winning races in both the MINI Miglia Championship and MINI Se7en Challenge, Selby originally represented NAPA Racing at the 2022 edition of the Goodwood Festival of Speed before then going on to race a NAPA-branded car in the EnduroKA series.

Making the high-profile switch to the MINI CHALLENGE in 2023, Selby entered the JCW class with the backing of NAPA Racing UK and punched in a number of standout performances to eventually seal the coveted Directors Cup.

Continuing to improve as each season has passed, the Buckinghamshire racer now takes on a brand-new challenge as he makes the step up to the BTCC.

Undoubtedly set to be one to watch in 2026, Selby will be looking to utilise all of his experience in front-wheel-drive machinery to hit the ground running and challenge for honours in the Jack Sears Trophy, which is for drivers who are yet to score more than one podium in the series.

drivers.name: Aiden Moffat
drivers.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Aiden Moffat is a Scottish racing driver born on 28 September 1996 in Dalkeith. He competes in the British Touring Car Championship and is known for his long-standing presence in the series and steady progression through the grid.

Moffat made history in 2013 when he debuted in the BTCC aged just 16, becoming the youngest driver ever to start a race in the championship. Over the years, he has developed into a consistent competitor, achieving multiple race wins and podium finishes, with a best championship result of eighth in 2021.

He has raced for a variety of teams during his career, including his family-run Laser Tools Racing outfit, and has driven several different cars and manufacturers. Known for his adaptability, Moffat is one of the few drivers to have won BTCC races in both front-wheel-drive and rear-wheel-drive machinery.

history.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts Racing with WSR
LKQ Euro Car Parts with SYNETIQ

drivers.name: Dexter Patterson
drivers.team: Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Dexter Patterson is a Scottish racing driver born on 16 August 2003 in Bathgate, West Lothian. He began his career in karting, where he achieved international success, including winning the CIK-FIA Karting European Championship in 2017.

Patterson progressed to single-seater racing, competing in series such as the F4 British Championship and the Euroformula Open Championship, before moving into touring cars. He joined the British Touring Car Championship in 2022, where he quickly showed pace and potential against more experienced drivers.

Known for his smooth driving style and strong racecraft, Patterson is considered one of the promising young talents in British motorsport, continuing to build experience and results in the highly competitive BTCC field.

history.team: Re.Beverages and Bartercard with Team HARD

drivers.name: Max Buxton
drivers.bio:
Max Buxton is a British racing driver who has competed in junior touring car categories and has progressed through UK saloon car racing on his path into higher-level competition.

He has made appearances in the British Touring Car Championship paddock environment, gaining experience in one of the UK’s most competitive motorsport series.

Buxton is regarded as an emerging talent within British motorsport, continuing to develop his racecraft and consistency as he builds his career in touring cars.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA

drivers.name: Chris Smiley
drivers.bio:
Chris Smiley is a Northern Irish racing driver born on 24 August 1995 in Larne, Northern Ireland. He began his career in karting before progressing through junior single-seater and touring car categories, quickly establishing himself as a competitive touring car talent.

Smiley made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2016 and has since raced for several teams, including BTC Racing. He achieved his first BTCC race win in 2020 at Silverstone, marking a major milestone in his career and confirming his pace at the front of the field.

Known for his aggressive but calculated driving style, Smiley has become a consistent podium contender in the BTCC and remains a respected competitor within the touring car paddock.

drivers.name: Dan Cammish
drivers.bio:
Dan Cammish is a British racing driver born on 10 February 1989 in Leeds, England. He began his career in karting before moving into single-seaters, where he won the British Formula Ford Championship in 2014 with a dominant season.

Cammish later transitioned into touring cars, joining the British Touring Car Championship in 2018 with Team Dynamics. He quickly established himself as a front-running driver, taking race wins, podiums, and pole positions, and becoming a consistent contender in both front-wheel-drive and rear-wheel-drive machinery.

Known for his smooth, precise driving style and strong qualifying pace, Cammish is widely regarded as one of the most technically skilled drivers on the BTCC grid.

drivers.name: Nicolas Hamilton
drivers.bio:
Nicolas Hamilton is a British racing driver born on 28 March 1992 in Stevenage, Hertfordshire, and the half-brother of seven-time Formula One World Champion Lewis Hamilton. He is the first disabled driver to compete in the BTCC, racing with cerebral palsy, which affects his lower body and requires him to use hand controls on the throttle and brake.

Hamilton made his BTCC debut in 2015 with AmD Tuning.com as a guest driver, making five appearances in an Audi S3. He has returned to the series on multiple occasions since, racking up over 160 race starts and becoming one of the most recognisable faces in British motorsport.

For 2026, Hamilton secures his most competitive drive yet — joining the Excelr8 Motorsport setup backed by Draper Tools to race a Hyundai i30 Fastback N Performance alongside a team capable of fighting for wins.

history.team: ROKiT Racing with iQuoto Online Trading

drivers.name: Daniel Rowbottom
drivers.bio:
Daniel Rowbottom is a British racing driver born on 10 October 1988 in Worcester, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing through various club-level racing categories and saloon car series, steadily building experience in touring cars.

Rowbottom made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2019 and quickly adapted to the highly competitive series. Driving for BTC Racing, he scored his first BTCC race win in 2021 at Croft, marking a breakthrough moment in his career.

Known for his determined and aggressive driving style, Rowbottom has developed into a consistent front-running competitor, regularly fighting for podiums and race wins in the BTCC.

drivers.name: Adam Morgan
drivers.bio:
Adam Morgan is a British racing driver born on 30 June 1994 in Preston, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing through junior saloon car racing, including success in the Ginetta Junior Championship where he became champion in 2010.

Morgan made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2012 and quickly established himself as a competitive midfield runner, later progressing to regular podium contention. He achieved his first BTCC race win in 2016 at Knockhill and has since added multiple podium finishes to his record.

Known for his consistency and racecraft, Morgan has become a respected and experienced driver in the BTCC paddock, often maximising results through strong strategy and tyre management.

history.team: Carlube TripleR Racing with Mac Tools

drivers.name: Árón Taylor-Smith
drivers.team: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
drivers.bio:
Árón Taylor-Smith is an Irish racing driver born on 30 June 1989 in Dublin, Ireland. He began his motorsport career in karting before moving into touring cars, progressing through the British and international touring car scene.

Taylor-Smith made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2011 and quickly built a reputation as a hard-fighting and determined racer. He has also competed in the World Touring Car Championship, gaining valuable experience on the global stage.

Over his BTCC career, he has achieved multiple race wins and podiums, including standout performances in independent and overall classifications. Known for his aggressive yet controlled racing style, Taylor-Smith remains a consistent and experienced competitor in touring car racing.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA
Team HARD. with HUB Financial Solutions

drivers.name: Gordon Shedden
drivers.team: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
drivers.bio:
Gordon Shedden is a Scottish racing driver born on 15 February 1979 in Edinburgh. He is one of the most successful drivers in British Touring Car Championship history, having made his debut in 2001 and becoming a consistent front-runner across two decades.

Shedden won the BTCC Drivers’ Championship three times (2012, 2015, and 2016), all with Honda machinery under Team Dynamics. During this period, he was part of one of the most dominant eras for a manufacturer in the series.

Known for his smooth yet highly competitive driving style, Shedden has amassed numerous race wins and podiums throughout his career and is widely regarded as one of the modern greats of touring car racing in the UK.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA

drivers.name: Josh Cook
drivers.bio:
Josh Cook is a British racing driver born on 1 May 1991 in Bath, England. He began his career in karting before moving into car racing through junior saloon car categories, quickly building a reputation for speed and racecraft.

Cook made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2017 and soon established himself as a race winner. He has taken multiple BTCC victories and podiums across different teams, including BTC Racing and One Motorsport, often proving especially strong in wet and mixed-condition races.

Known for his aggressive, entertaining driving style and strong overtaking ability, Cook is widely regarded as one of the most exciting and competitive drivers in the modern BTCC field.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA
LKQ Euro Car Parts with SYNETIQ
Team Parker Racing with Maximum Motorsport

drivers.name: Sam Osborne
drivers.bio:
Sam Osborne is a British racing driver born on 29 April 1997 in Oxfordshire, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing into junior racing categories, including the Renault Clio Cup UK, where he developed his touring car skills.

Osborne made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2020 and has since competed for Motorbase Performance and other BTCC teams. He has steadily built experience in the series, becoming a reliable midfield competitor known for consistency and clean racing.

While still developing his front-running results, Osborne is recognised for his improving pace and strong racecraft as he continues to establish himself in one of the UK’s most competitive motorsport championships.

history.team: MB Motorsport accelerated by Blue Square

drivers.name: Tom Ingram
drivers.bio:
Tom Ingram is a British racing driver born on 15 June 1993 in High Wycombe, England. He began his career in karting before moving into the Ginetta Junior Championship, where he quickly established himself as a front-running talent.

Ingram progressed into touring cars through the BTCC support series, winning the Renault Clio Cup UK in 2014. He stepped up to the British Touring Car Championship in 2016 and rapidly became one of the series’ leading drivers, consistently fighting at the front of the grid.

He won the BTCC Drivers’ Championship in 2022 after a highly competitive season with Excelr8 Motorsport. Known for his smooth driving style, consistency, and strong tyre management, Ingram is widely regarded as one of the top modern touring car drivers in the UK.

history.team: Bristol Street Motors with Excelr8 TradePriceCars.com
Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with Ginsters

drivers.name: Mikey Doble
drivers.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Mikey Doble is a British racing driver born on 12 September 1999 in London, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing into saloon car racing, where he built experience in junior touring car categories and club-level championships.

Doble made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2023, stepping up into one of the UK’s most competitive motorsport series. Since joining the BTCC, he has focused on developing consistency, racecraft, and pace against a highly experienced field of drivers.

Known for his steady progression and professionalism, Doble continues to establish himself in touring cars as he builds experience and works towards stronger midfield and front-running results.

history.team: Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing

teams.name: Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing
Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing

drivers.name: Charles Rainford
drivers.bio:
Charles Rainford is a British racing driver born on 20 July 1998 in Horsham, West Sussex. After years competing in historic motorsport and the Porsche Carrera Cup GB — where he claimed the Pro-Am title in 2022 and finished runner-up overall in 2024 — Rainford stepped up to the BTCC with West Surrey Racing in 2025.

His debut season was nothing short of outstanding. He took a maiden BTCC win in just his sixth start at Brands Hatch Indy, added a pole position at Knockhill, and finished eighth in the Drivers' Championship. He was also voted the 2025 Fans' Choice Award winner.

For 2026, Rainford stays with WSR and is widely regarded as one of the most exciting young talents in British touring car racing, with genuine championship ambitions ahead.

history.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts Racing with WSR

drivers.name: Ashley Sutton
drivers.bio:
Ashley Sutton is a British racing driver born on 15 January 1994 in Stoke-on-Trent, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing into junior saloon car racing, quickly showing strong pace and racecraft.

Sutton made his British Touring Car Championship debut in 2016 and immediately impressed, winning the BTCC Drivers’ Championship in his rookie season. He went on to win further titles in 2020 and 2023, establishing himself as one of the most successful modern drivers in the series.

Known for his aggressive yet controlled driving style, adaptability, and ability to perform under pressure, Sutton has become a multiple-time BTCC champion and one of the standout talents in British touring car racing.

drivers.name: James Dorlin
drivers.bio:
James Dorlin is a British racing driver born on 22 May 1999 in Barnsley, England. He began his motorsport career in karting before progressing into junior saloon car racing, where he quickly emerged as a front-running talent.

Dorlin won the 2016 Ginetta Junior Championship, marking him out as one of the UK’s leading young prospects. He then moved into GT and endurance racing, including campaigns in the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and British GT, where he developed strong racecraft, consistency, and technical adaptability across different cars and race formats.

In 2025, Dorlin stepped up to the British Touring Car Championship, joining the BTCC grid after several successful seasons in GT machinery. His move represents a transition into one of the most competitive touring car series in the world.

Known for his smooth driving style and versatility, Dorlin is regarded as an adaptable racer aiming to establish himself quickly within the BTCC midfield and progress toward front-running results.

history.team: TOYOTA GAZOO Racing UK with IAA

teams.name: NAPA Racing UK
teams.bio:
Operating under the Motorbase Performance banner, NAPA Racing UK are one of the most formidable outfits in the BTCC paddock. Founded in 2006 by David Bartrum in Sittingbourne, Kent, the team have built a reputation for extracting the maximum from Ford-powered machinery over two decades of competition.

The squad claimed back-to-back Drivers' and Teams' Championships in 2024 and 2025 with Jake Hill and Ashley Sutton respectively, cementing their status as the dominant force in the modern era. Their 2025 title haul, a clean sweep of Drivers', Teams' and Manufacturers' titles, was the team's most complete season to date.

For 2026 the team field four cars under the NAPA Auto Parts livery, running a quartet of highly experienced racers led by two-time champion Ashley Sutton. The distinctive yellow and blue colour scheme has become one of the most recognised sights in British motorsport.

teams.name: Team VERTU
teams.bio:
Run by the award-winning EXCELR8 Motorsport, Team VERTU are backed by one of the UK's largest motor retail groups. EXCELR8 entered the BTCC in 2018 and rapidly grew into championship contenders, earning their first major drivers' titles in 2022 and 2025 when Tom Ingram claimed the Drivers' Championship.

Based in Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire, the team have developed a fierce reputation for consistently fast and reliable Hyundai i30N Fastback machinery. Ingram remained their spearhead throughout their Hyundai era before the operation transitioned to new machinery for 2026.

The VERTU Motors sponsorship has given the squad significant commercial backing, and the team's data-led approach under team principal Justina Williams has seen them become one of the best-resourced teams on the grid. With multiple championship wins across different categories, EXCELR8's move into BTCC has been one of the success stories of the past decade.

teams.name: Speedworks Corolla Racing
teams.bio:
Speedworks Motorsport are the team behind the Toyota Corolla GR Sport BTCC programme, and one of the most respected independent operations in British touring car racing. Founded in 2010 and based in Middlewich, Cheshire, they made their full-time BTCC debut in 2013 before growing steadily into title contenders.

The team's crowning moment came in 2022 when Tom Ingram, their long-serving lead driver, won the Drivers' Championship after years of consistent front-running. That title validated years of careful development and cemented Speedworks' standing as a major force in the series.

Running the Toyota Corolla GR Sport under the GR Sport Corolla Racing banner, Speedworks benefit from a strong manufacturer relationship with Toyota GB and are one of the few teams to develop and run their own car from the ground up. Their Middlewich base is home to a meticulous engineering operation that punches well above its weight relative to its size.

teams.name: West Surrey Racing
teams.bio:
West Surrey Racing are the most decorated and longest-running team in the BTCC. Founded in 1979 by Dick Bennetts, the Wokingham-based outfit have been a constant presence in British motorsport for over four decades, fielding cars for champions including Steve Soper, Will Hoy, James Thompson, Colin Turkington and Rob Collard.

The team's association with BMW stretches back many years and they are regarded as the factory BMW partner in the BTCC, running the iconic 330e M Sport and latterly the 128ti. Their engineering department is widely considered to be among the finest in the paddock and they have historically been the benchmark against which rival teams measure themselves.

With nine Drivers' Championship victories and five Teams' titles to their name, WSR's record is unmatched in the modern era of the championship. Colin Turkington alone won four titles with the team, making him the most successful driver in BTCC history. The team continues to attract blue-chip sponsorship and remains one of the best-funded and best-resourced operations in the series.

teams.name: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
teams.bio:
Power Maxed Racing are one of the most ambitious independent teams in the BTCC, based at their Coventry headquarters in the heart of the UK's motorsport corridor. Shaun Hollamby's team made their BTCC debut in 2010 and have spent subsequent years steadily building their technical capability.

The team have campaigned a variety of machinery over the years, most recently switching to the Audi S3 Saloon as part of the Goodyear-sponsored PMR programme. Their Coventry base houses an in-house engineering and fabrication department, allowing the team to react quickly to the demands of the BTCC's intensive race calendar.

Power Maxed are known for nurturing young British talent and have given a number of drivers their first full-time BTCC opportunities. The team's stated ambition is to secure their first outright championship title, and they continue to develop their package in pursuit of that goal.

teams.name: Cataclean Plato Racing
teams.bio:
Plato Racing is the team founded by BTCC legend Jason Plato, one of the most successful drivers in the history of the championship with two Drivers' titles and over 100 race wins to his name. After retiring from full-time driving, Plato channelled his experience and passion for the series into running his own outfit.

Based in Kidlington, Oxfordshire, the team campaign Mercedes-AMG A35 Saloon machinery and represent one of the most intriguing projects on the 2026 grid. Plato's hands-on involvement, drawing on his decades of experience as a front-running driver, gives the team a unique perspective on car setup and driver coaching.

With Plato's unrivalled knowledge of what it takes to win in the BTCC, the team are building methodically towards the front of the field. Their Mercedes-AMG represents one of the newer platforms in the series, and Plato Racing are working hard to develop the package into a consistent championship challenger.

teams.name: Restart Racing
teams.bio:
Restart Racing carry forward a storied history in the modern BTCC, operating from their base in Didcot, Oxfordshire. The team were originally known as BTC Racing, later running as Rich Energy BTC Racing, before rebranding as Restart Racing ahead of their switch to Hyundai machinery.

Under their BTC Racing identity, the team became one of the series' top outfits, consistently challenging for race wins and podiums with drivers including Josh Cook and Jake Hill. The operation rebranded as Restart Racing ahead of their switch to Hyundai machinery for 2026, marking a new chapter in their professional development.

Founded in 2000, the team have accumulated over two decades of BTCC experience and have never been far from the sharp end of the championship. For 2026 the Restart Racing brand represents a fresh identity, and the team are well-positioned to challenge at the front of the grid with experienced drivers and strong manufacturer support.

teams.name: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
teams.bio: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport are a competitive independent outfit campaigning Toyota Corolla GR Sport machinery in the BTCC. Backed by Laser Tools, one of the UK's leading hand tool suppliers, the team's partnership with MB Motorsport brings proven BTCC expertise to the operation.

teams.name: Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing
teams.bio:
Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing is the second BTCC entry run by Shaun Hollamby's Coventry-based Power Maxed Racing operation, carrying the Steel Seal head gasket repair brand as title sponsor. PMR have been a fixture in the BTCC since 2010, and their multi-car programme for 2026 reflects the team's growing ambition and commercial support.

Running Audi A3 Saloon machinery, the Steel Seal entry campaigns alongside their LKQ Euro Car Parts-backed sister car as part of a coordinated effort to score as many constructor points as possible across the season.
